condition: DiagnosticReport.code (LOINC) resolves to OMOP concept with domain_id = Measurement
narrative_md: When a DiagnosticReport's LOINC code resolves to an OMOP concept with domain_id = Measurement, the report itself produces one or more measurement rows. Each conclusionCode entry generates a separate row. This is independent of the Observation results referenced by DiagnosticReport.result[], which are mapped by the Observation mapper. Lab panel codes (e.g. 24323-8 Comprehensive metabolic panel) are the primary Measurement-domain codes.

fields.notes: Surrogate key. Hash/sequence of DiagnosticReport.id + conclusionCode index. Must be unique across all measurement sources.
Resolve Patient/{id} reference to integer person_id. Skip row if unresolvable.
LOINC code looked up in OMOP vocabulary. Must have domain_id = Measurement. Use first LOINC coding found. 0 if concept not found.
Date component of effectiveDateTime, or effectivePeriod.start. Skip row if absent (ETL-German behavior).

sources.comment: Skip resource if effective[x] absent; accepts effectiveDateTime or effectivePeriod.start
Uses effectivePeriod.start (parser extracts but does not map to OMOP)

fields.notes: Full timestamp from effectiveDateTime or effectivePeriod.start.
Legacy field. Leave null.
ETL-German maps category via SOURCE_VOCABULARY_ID_DIAGNOSTIC_REPORT_CATEGORY custom concept map. Common defaults: 32817 (EHR) or 32856 (Lab result).

sources.comment: Custom source_to_concept_map keyed on 'DiagnosticReport_Category'; LAB→32856, others→32817
Field listed as required but left null in skeleton
Conceptual guidance: map category code to type concept

fields.notes: ETL-German maps SNOMED conclusion interpretation codes to operator. Typically null for DiagnosticReport.
DiagnosticReport conclusions are coded, not numeric. Leave null.
SNOMED conclusion code resolved to OMOP concept. ETL-German currently stores this in measurement_source_concept_id instead.

sources.comment: Not used; SNOMED conclusionCode concept stored in measurement_source_concept_id instead (line 339)
Conceptual guidance: conclusionCode should populate value_as_concept_id

fields.notes: No units on DiagnosticReport-level measurements. Leave null.
No reference ranges on DiagnosticReport. Leave null.
No reference ranges on DiagnosticReport. Leave null.
First performer reference resolved to provider_id. Also consider resultsInterpreter[0].
Resolve Encounter/{id} to visit_occurrence_id.
Leave null unless visit details are modeled.
Verbatim SNOMED code from conclusionCode. ETL-German uses this field.
SNOMED conclusionCode resolved to OMOP concept ID. ETL-German stores the SNOMED concept here (line 339).

sources.comment: SNOMED conclusionCode concept stored here instead of value_as_concept_id (line 339); splits composite SNOMED expressions

fields.notes: No units. Leave null.
No units. Leave null.
Verbatim SNOMED code. ETL-German stores this (line 344).
Can link back to a related OMOP record if needed. Leave null.
Leave null.

references.notes: Primary reference. createDiagnosticReportMeasurement() with full domain routing, LOINC lookup, SNOMED conclusionCode, composite SNOMED splitting.
Skeleton mapping with all 23 measurement fields listed but all set to null.
Parser only -- extracts code, status, effectivePeriod. No OMOP output mapping.
Conceptual mapping guidance. Documents domain-based routing and field correspondences.

edge_cases.handling: ETL-German skips the resource entirely. Alternative: fall back to issued timestamp.

edge_cases.case: Status is registered, preliminary, cancelled, entered-in-error, or unknown
edge_cases.handling: Reject. Accept only final, amended, corrected, appended. Preliminary results may change and should not be committed to the CDM.
ETL-German skips the resource. Without a conclusion, there is no value to store. Only the referenced Observations produce rows.
Each code produces a separate measurement row. The measurement_concept_id (report LOINC) is the same across all rows; only measurement_source_concept_id and value_source_value differ.
ETL-German splits composite expressions: base code before : maps to measurement_source_concept_id; attributes after :{ are parsed for interpretation codes mapped to operator_concept_id.
ETL-German splits on + and creates one row per component code.
ETL-German skips the resource. Alternative: default to measurement_type_concept_id = 32817 (EHR).

edge_cases.case: LOINC code resolves to Observation or Procedure domain
edge_cases.handling: Route to observation or procedure_occurrence table instead.

edge_cases.case: Report with result[] but no code or conclusionCode
edge_cases.handling: No report-level row produced. The Observations in result[] are still mapped independently.
Not supported. OMOP requires a single person_id. Skip the resource.
ETL-German deletes existing OMOP rows by fhirLogicalId or fhirIdentifier before re-inserting.
